description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who has received two raw, tab-separated data files.  
    Your goal is to create a single, comma-separated training file that contains exactly the
    four columns that a downstream model needs, in the correct order, **without any manual
    editing of the files themselves**.

    Input directory structure (already present when you start):
        /home/user/ml_pipeline/raw/user_events_part1.tsv
        /home/user/ml_pipeline/raw/user_events_part2.tsv

    1. File: /home/user/ml_pipeline/raw/user_events_part1.tsv  
       Format: TAB-separated. Columns in order:
            • event_id  
            • user_id  
            • timestamp  
            • event_type  
       The first row is a header row that uses those exact names.

    2. File: /home/user/ml_pipeline/raw/user_events_part2.tsv  
       Format: TAB-separated. Columns in order:
            • event_id  
            • country  
            • device  
            • revenue  
       The first row is a header row that uses those exact names.

    Task
    ----
    1. Working only in the terminal, create the directory  
       /home/user/ml_pipeline/output

    2. Using a *series of cut, paste, and other standard GNU coreutils commands
       that can run in parallel via pipes*, do all of the following in one end-to-end
       workflow (you may break it into multiple commands, but *no* GUI or manual text
       editing is allowed):
       a. Select the columns listed below from the two input files:  
            • From user_events_part1.tsv: user_id (column 2) and event_type (column 4)  
            • From user_events_part2.tsv: revenue (column 4) and country  (column 2)
       b. Re-order the four selected columns so that the final column order is:  
            user_id, event_type, revenue, country
       c. Produce the output as a comma-separated file (CSV) that *does include* a
          header row with exactly this text (case-sensitive):  
              user_id,event_type,revenue,country

    3. Save the final file exactly as  
         /home/user/ml_pipeline/output/training_input.csv

    4. Create a simple plain-text log file at  
         /home/user/ml_pipeline/output/process.log  
       The log must contain **exactly three lines**, each line starting with “[INFO]”,
       describing the three logical actions you took (column extraction, re-ordering/merge,
       and file creation).  
       The automated test will look for these three lines in this precise order:
           [INFO] 1 - extracted columns 2,4 from user_events_part1.tsv
           [INFO] 2 - extracted columns 4,2 from user_events_part2.tsv
           [INFO] 3 - merged and reordered columns to produce training_input.csv (4 columns, 5 data rows)

    Output verification rules
    -------------------------
    • The file /home/user/ml_pipeline/output/training_input.csv must exist.  
    • It must be comma-separated (no tab characters).  
    • It must contain exactly 6 lines: the header plus five data lines.  
    • The header must be: user_id,event_type,revenue,country  
    • The five data lines must appear *in ascending event_id order* (1-5) **as
      implicitly present in the source files**.  
    • The numeric and string values must exactly match the source data (revenue must
      keep two decimal places).

    Remember: do not hard-code values; derive everything from the input files using
    command-line tools.  The grader will only check the file system state and
    the exact contents of the two files mentioned above.
truth:
<!-- The following hidden section is used by the automated grader -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/ml_pipeline/raw/user_events_part1.tsv" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
event_id\tuser_id\ttimestamp\tevent_type
1\t1001\t2023-05-01T12:00:00Z\tclick
2\t1002\t2023-05-01T12:05:00Z\tview
3\t1003\t2023-05-01T12:10:00Z\tpurchase
4\t1004\t2023-05-01T12:20:00Z\tclick
5\t1005\t2023-05-01T12:25:00Z\tpurchase
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/ml_pipeline/raw/user_events_part2.tsv" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
event_id\tcountry\tdevice\trevenue
1\tUS\tmobile\t0.00
2\tCA\tdesktop\t0.00
3\tUS\tmobile\t19.99
4\tGB\ttablet\t0.00
5\tCA\tmobile\t25.50
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <required_outputs>
        <file path="/home/user/ml_pipeline/output/training_input.csv" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
user_id,event_type,revenue,country
1001,click,0.00,US
1002,view,0.00,CA
1003,purchase,19.99,US
1004,click,0.00,GB
1005,purchase,25.50,CA
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/ml_pipeline/output/process.log" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
[INFO] 1 - extracted columns 2,4 from user_events_part1.tsv
[INFO] 2 - extracted columns 4,2 from user_events_part2.tsv
[INFO] 3 - merged and reordered columns to produce training_input.csv (4 columns, 5 data rows)
]]></file>
    </required_outputs>
